question.label: Self-read opener
question.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
question.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

answer: I think I am shipping, but I am not sure I am aiming at the highest-leverage work yet.

assessment.note: He names a clear self-read gap in targeting highest-leverage work, which is a strong clarity concern alongside continued shipping investment.

new_queue.label: Highest-leverage work
new_queue.name: You said 'highest-leverage work' — what work specifically are you pointing at?

new_queue.label: What they're proud of
new_queue.name: What's something from the last quarter you're genuinely proud of — not what looks good, what actually was good?

new_queue.label: Principal scope signal
new_queue.name: What does Principal scope mean on this team in practice?

new_queue.label: Gap awareness
new_queue.name: Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?

new_queue.label: Expectation alignment
new_queue.name: When a cross-team dependency is shaky, how do you set expectations early?

new_queue.label: Tradeoff calls
new_queue.name: Where are you making the tradeoff call yourself instead of pushing it up?

new_queue.label: Escalation threshold
new_queue.name: What makes you decide to escalate versus keep driving it yourself?

new_queue.label: Next commitment
new_queue.name: What is the first concrete change you want to make before our next review?

history.answer_excerpt: I think I am shipping, but I am not sure I am aiming at the highest-leverage work yet.
I think I am shipping, but I am not sure I am aiming at the highest-leverage work yet.
